_meta.description: Fixed liturgical texts for the Lamentations — Matins of Great and Holy Saturday, served Friday evening
_meta.translation: St. Sergius of Radonezh Cathedral (based on Lenten Triodion)
_meta.note: The Lamentations (Epitaphios service) center on three stases of verses sung around the burial shroud of Christ. Psalm 118 (119) verses are interspersed with composed troparia. The service concludes with a procession with the Epitaphios.
_meta._audit: v0.3.0: Full Lamentations text updated to match st-sergius.org/services/triod/76.pdf. Psalm verses, troparia, evlogetaria, kontakion/ikos, and small litany exclamations aligned to St. Sergius translation. Structural issues (malformed last-verse troparia) fixed. Soft-hyphen artifacts stripped. Typos corrected.

opening.exclamation: Blessed is our God always, now and ever and unto ages of ages.

sixPsalms._note: Psalms 3, 37, 62, 87, 102, 142. Read from psalter.json at assembly time.
sixPsalms.intro:
Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will among men. (×3)

O Lord, Thou shalt open my lips, and my mouth shall declare Thy praise. (×2)
sixPsalms.midGlory:
Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, now and ever and unto ages of ages. Amen.

Alleluia, alleluia, alleluia! Glory to Thee, O God! (×3)

Lord, have mercy. (×3)

Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, now and ever and unto ages of ages. Amen.

verses: God is the Lord, and hath appeared unto us; blessed is He that cometh in the Name of the Lord.
O give thanks unto the Lord, for He is good, for His mercy endureth forever.
All nations compassed me about, but in the Name of the Lord I cut them off.
This is the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous in our eyes.

nobleJoseph.label: Troparion
nobleJoseph.text:
Noble Joseph, taking down Thy most pure body from the Tree,
wrapped it in clean linen with sweet spices,
and he laid it in a new tomb.

glory.label: Troparion
glory.text:
When Thou didst descend unto death, O Life Immortal,
then didst Thou slay Hades with the radiant brilliance of Thy Divinity.
And when Thou didst also raise the dead out of the nethermost depths,
all the Hosts of the heavens cried out:
"O Life-giver, Christ our God, glory be to Thee."

now.label: Theotokion
now.text:
The Angel stood by the tomb, and to the women bearing spices he cried aloud:
"Myrrh is fitting for the dead,
but Christ hath shown Himself a stranger to corruption."

stasis1._note: Stasis 1 — Tone 5. Psalm 118:1-72 (Aleph-Teth) with troparia. 73 pairs + Glory + Now. Translation: St. Sergius of Radonezh Cathedral.
stasis1.refrain: Thou who art Life wast laid in a tomb, O Christ; and the hosts of angels were amazed and glorified Thy condescension.

verses.psalm: Blessed are the blameless in the way, who walk in the law of the Lord.
verses.troparion: Thou who art Life wast laid in a tomb, O Christ; and the hosts of angels were amazed and glorified Thy condescension.

verses.psalm: Blessed are they that search out His testimonies: they will diligently seek Him with the whole heart.
verses.troparion: O Life, how canst Thou die? How canst Thou dwell in a tomb? Yet Thou dost destroy death's kingdom and raise the dead from Hades.

verses.psalm: For they that work iniquity have not walked in His ways.
verses.troparion: We magnify Thee, Jesus our King: we honor Thy burial and Thy sufferings, whereby Thou hast saved us from corruption.

verses.psalm: Thou hast commanded us diligently to keep Thy precepts.
verses.troparion: Thou who hast bound the earth, dost go this day to dwell in a small tomb, raising up the dead from their tombs.

verses.psalm: O that my ways were directed to keep Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: O Jesus, my Christ and King of all, why hast Thou come to those in Hades? Is it to set free the race of mortal man?

verses.psalm: Then shall I not be ashamed, when I have respect to all Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: The Master of all is seen lying dead, and laid in a new tomb, He who hath emptied the tombs of the reposed.

verses.psalm: I will give Thee thanks with uprightness of heart, when I have learnt the judgments of Thy righteousness.
verses.troparion: Thou who art Life wast laid in a tomb, O Christ: by Thy death Thou didst destroy death, and become a fount of life for the world.

verses.psalm: I will keep Thine ordinances: O forsake me not greatly.
verses.troparion: Numbered with the transgressors, O Christ, Thou hast redeemed us all from the guilt brought upon us of old by the deceiver.

verses.psalm: Wherewith shall a young man direct his way? by keeping Thy words.
verses.troparion: Fairer in His beauty than all mortal men, He hath appeared now as a corpse without form or comeliness, He who hath rendered the nature of all things comely.

verses.psalm: With my whole heart have I diligently sought Thee: cast me not away from Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: How could Hades endure Thy coming, O Savior? Was it not shattered and struck blind by the brilliant radiance of Thy light?

verses.psalm: I have hidden Thine oracles in my heart, that I might not sin against Thee.
verses.troparion: O Jesus, my sweetness and light of salvation, how art Thou hidden in a dark tomb? O patience ineffable, transcending speech!

verses.psalm: Blessed art Thou, O Lord: teach me Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: The spiritual powers and the angelic hosts are amazed, O Christ, at the ineffable mystery of Thy burial, beyond speech.

verses.psalm: With my lips have I declared all the judgments of Thy mouth.
verses.troparion: O strange and new wonder! He who hath granted me the breath of life is carried lifeless in Joseph's arms to burial.

verses.psalm: I have delighted in the way of Thy testimonies, as much as in all riches.
verses.troparion: Thou hast descended into the tomb, O Christ, yet wast never parted from Thy Father's side. O strange and wondrous marvel!

verses.psalm: I will meditate on Thy commandments, and consider Thy ways.
verses.troparion: Though Thou wast shut within the smallest of sepulchers, O Jesus, all creation knew Thee as the true King of heaven and earth.

verses.psalm: I will meditate on Thine ordinances: I will not forget Thy words.
verses.troparion: When Thou wast laid in a tomb, O Christ the Creator, the foundations of Hades were shaken and the graves of mortal men were opened.

verses.psalm: Render a recompence to Thy servant: so shall I live, and keep Thy words.
verses.troparion: He who holds the earth in the palm of His hand is held fast by the earth; put to death according to the flesh, delivering the dead from the grasping hand of Hades.

verses.psalm: Unveil Thou mine eyes, and I shall perceive wondrous things out of Thy law.
verses.troparion: Having died O Savior, Thou didst leave this life, and coming to those who have reposed, Thou didst shatter the gates of Hades.

verses.psalm: I am a stranger in the earth: hide not Thy commandments from me.
verses.troparion: The flesh of God is hidden now beneath the earth, like a candle underneath a bushel, driving away the darkness in Hades.

verses.psalm: My soul has longed exceedingly for Thy judgments at all times.
verses.troparion: The multitude of heavenly hosts make haste with Joseph and Nicodemus to bury Thee, whom nothing can contain, in a small tomb.

verses.psalm: Thou hast rebuked the proud: cursed are they that turn aside from Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: Of Thine own will Thou wast slain and laid beneath the earth, O my Jesus. Thou Fount of Life, Who hath brought me, who was dead through bitter sin, back to life.

verses.psalm: Remove from me reproach and contempt; for I have sought out Thy testimonies.
verses.troparion: The whole of creation was transformed by Thy Passion: for all things suffered with Thee, knowing, O Word, that Thou art the Fashioner of all that is.

verses.psalm: For princes sat and spoke against me: but Thy servant was meditating on Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: All-devouring Hades received within itself the Rock of Life, and spewed forth all the dead that it had swallowed from the beginning of the ages.

verses.psalm: For Thy testimonies are my meditation, and Thine ordinances are my counsellors.
verses.troparion: Thou wast laid in a new tomb, O Christ, and thereby renewed the nature of mortal man, wondrously arising from the dead.

verses.psalm: My soul has cleaved to the ground; quicken Thou me according to Thy word.
verses.troparion: Thou didst descend to the earth, to save Adam: not leaving him on earth, O Master, but even descending into Hades to seek him.

verses.psalm: I declared my ways, and Thou didst hear me; teach me Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: The whole earth quaked with fear, O Word, and the daystar hid its rays, when Thy Light was hidden in the earth.

verses.psalm: Instruct me in the way of Thine ordinances; and I will meditate on Thy wondrous works.
verses.troparion: Willingly didst Thou die as a mortal man, O Savior, but as God Thou hast raised from the dead, those who were sunk in the depths of sin.

verses.psalm: My soul has slumbered for sorrow; strengthen Thou me with Thy words.
verses.troparion: The pure Virgin shed tears of lamentation over Thee, Jesus, and with a mother's grief she cried: "How shall I bury Thee, my Son?"

verses.psalm: Remove from me the way of iniquity; and be merciful to me by Thy law.
verses.troparion: Buried in the earth like a grain of wheat, Thou hast yielded a rich harvest, raising to life the mortal sons of Adam.

verses.psalm: I have chosen the way of truth; and have not forgotten Thy judgments.
verses.troparion: Now art Thou hidden beneath the earth like the setting sun and covered by the night of death: but do Thou dawn most brightly O Savior.

verses.psalm: I have cleaved to Thy testimonies, O Lord; put me not to shame.
verses.troparion: As the moon hideth the disk of the sun, O Savior, so now hath the grave hidden Thee, bodily eclipsed in death.

verses.psalm: I ran the way of Thy commandments, when Thou didst enlarge my heart.
verses.troparion: Christ the Life, by tasting death, hath delivered mortal mankind from death, giving life to all.

verses.psalm: Teach me, O Lord, the way of Thine ordinances, and I will seek it out continually.
verses.troparion: Adam was slain of old through envy, but by Thy repose Thou hast brought him back to life, O Savior, Who hath been revealed in the flesh as the new Adam.

verses.psalm: Instruct me, and I will search out Thy law, and will keep it with my whole heart.
verses.troparion: The ranks of noetic powers saw Thee, O Savior, laid out dead for our sake, and were filled with wonder, covering themselves with their noetic wings.

verses.psalm: Guide me in the path of Thy commandments; for I have delighted in it.
verses.troparion: Taking Thee down dead from the Tree, O Word, Joseph laid Thee in a tomb: but do Thou rise up now as God and save us all.

verses.psalm: Incline mine heart to Thy testimonies, and not to covetousness.
verses.troparion: Thou art the Joy of the angels, O Savior, but now Thou art become the cause of their grief, as they see Thee in the flesh a breathless corpse.

verses.psalm: Turn away mine eyes that I may not behold vanity: quicken Thou me in Thy way.
verses.troparion: Lifted up upon the Cross, Thou hast lifted up with Thyself all mankind; and descending beneath the earth, Thou hast raised all that lay therein.

verses.psalm: Confirm Thine oracle to Thy servant, that he may fear Thee.
verses.troparion: As a lion Thou didst fall asleep in the flesh, O Savior, and as a lion cub Thou didst arise from the dead, putting off the oldness of the flesh.

verses.psalm: Take away my reproach which I have feared: for Thy judgments are good.
verses.troparion: Thou Who didst fashion Eve from Adam's side, had Thy side pierced and from it ever floweth streams of cleansing.

verses.psalm: Behold, I have desired Thy commandments: quicken me in Thy righteousness.
verses.troparion: In secret the lamb of old was sacrificed; but Thou, O Savior, wast sacrificed openly and thereby cleansed the whole of creation.

verses.psalm: And let Thy mercy come upon me, O Lord; even Thy salvation, according to Thy word.
verses.troparion: Who can describe this strange and new manifestation? The Lord of Creation hath accepted the Passion today and hath died for our sake.

verses.psalm: And so I shall render an answer to them that reproach me: for I have trusted in Thy words.
verses.troparion: "How is it that we see the Giver of Life breathless?" Thus did the angels cry out in amazement. "How is it that God is enclosed within a tomb?"

verses.psalm: And take not the word of truth utterly out of my mouth; for I have hoped in Thy judgments.
verses.troparion: Pierced by a spear, O Savior, the life of the living issued forth from Thy side saving me who hath been banished from life, and quickening me with it.

verses.psalm: So shall I keep Thy law continually, for ever and ever.
verses.troparion: Stretched out upon the Tree, Thou hast drawn mortal mankind to unity; pierced in Thy life-giving side, O Jesus, Thou hast become a fount of forgiveness for all.

verses.psalm: I walked also at large: for I sought out Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: With fear and reverence didst the noble one prepare thy body for burial, O Savior, and with wonder beheld Thy fearful form.

verses.psalm: And I spoke of Thy testimonies before kings, and was not ashamed.
verses.troparion: Desiring to descend as one dead beneath the earth, O Jesus, Thou didst lead up the fallen from earth to heaven.

verses.psalm: And I meditated on Thy commandments, which I loved exceedingly.
verses.troparion: Seen as one Dead, yet alive as God, O Jesus, Thou didst lead up the fallen from earth to heaven.

verses.psalm: And I lifted up my hands to Thy commandments which I loved.
verses.troparion: Seen as one Dead, yet alive as God, O Jesus, Thou hast restored dead mortals to life and slain him who slew me.

verses.psalm: And I meditated in Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: How great the joy, how full the gladness, that Thou hast brought to those in Hades, flashing forth like lightning in its gloomy depths.

verses.psalm: Remember Thy words to Thy servant, wherein Thou hast made me hope.
verses.troparion: I venerate Thy Passion, I hymn Thy burial, and I magnify Thy power, O Lover of mankind: by which I have been set free from corrupting passions.

verses.psalm: This has comforted me in mine affliction: for Thine oracle has quickened me.
verses.troparion: A sword was sharpened against Thee, O Christ: but the sword of the strong hath been blunted, and the sword that guardeth Eden hath been turned back.

verses.psalm: The proud have transgressed exceedingly; but I swerved not from Thy law.
verses.troparion: The Ewe-lamb, seeing her Lamb slaughtered, was pierced with anguish: and she cried aloud in grief, calling the flock to lament with her.

verses.psalm: I remembered Thy judgments of old, O Lord; and was comforted.
verses.troparion: Even though Thou art buried in a grave, and hast descended into Hades, O Christ, Thou didst empty the graves therein and strip Hades naked.

verses.psalm: Despair took hold upon me, because of the sinners who forsake Thy law.
verses.troparion: Willingly, O Savior, hast Thou descended beneath the earth, restoring the dead to life, and leading them back to the glory of the Father.

verses.psalm: Thine ordinances were my songs in the place of my sojourning.
verses.troparion: One of the Trinity endureth a shameful death in the flesh for our sake; the sun trembleth and the earth doth quake.

verses.psalm: I remembered Thy name, O Lord, in the night, and kept Thy law.
verses.troparion: Offspring from a bitter source, the children of the tribe of Judah have cast into a pit Him who fed them with manna, Jesus.

verses.psalm: This I had, because I diligently sought Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: The Judge stood as one accused before the judgment-seat of Pilate, and He was condemned to an unjust death upon the Wood of the Cross.

verses.psalm: Thou art my portion, O Lord: I said that I would keep Thy law.
verses.troparion: Prideful Israel, O murderous people, why have ye set free Barabbas but delivered the Savior to be crucified?

verses.psalm: I besought Thy favour with my whole heart: have mercy upon me according to Thy word.
verses.troparion: With Thine hand Thou didst fashion Adam from the earth; and for his sake Thou hast assumed the nature of man, and of Thine own will been crucified.

verses.psalm: I thought on Thy ways, and turned my feet to Thy testimonies.
verses.troparion: Thou wast obedient to Thine own Father, O Word, even unto descending to evil Hades and raising up the race of mankind.

verses.psalm: I prepared myself, (and was not terrified,) to keep Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: "Woe is me, O Light of the world! Woe is me, O my Light! O Jesus, my heart's desire!" cried the Virgin in her bitter grief.

verses.psalm: The snares of sinners entangled me: but I forgot not Thy law.
verses.troparion: O jealous, bloodthirsty and proud people! May the very grave-clothes and the napkin put you to shame upon the Resurrection of Christ.

verses.psalm: At midnight I arose, to give thanks to Thee for the judgments of Thy righteousness.
verses.troparion: Come, evil murderous disciple of thy Lord, and show me the manner of thy wickedness, how thou hast become Christ's betrayer.

verses.psalm: I am a companion of all them that fear Thee, and of them that keep Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: None have believed thy pretense of loving mankind, O blind, utterly merciless, and faithless one, for thou didst sell for a price the sweet Myrrh.

verses.psalm: O Lord, the earth is full of Thy mercy: teach me Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: What price hast thou received for the heavenly Myrrh? What wast thou given in exchange for Him who is exceedingly precious? Thou hast procured for thyself madness, O accursed Satan.

verses.psalm: Thou hast wrought kindly with Thy servant, O Lord, according to Thy word.
verses.troparion: If thou lovest the poor and dost grieve over the ointment emptied out for the cleansing of a soul, how is it that thou hast sold the Giver of Light for gold?

verses.psalm: Teach me kindness, and instruction, and knowledge: for I have believed Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: "O my God and Word, my Joy, how shall I endure Thy three days in the tomb? For my heart is torn in pieces by a mother's grief."

verses.psalm: Before I was afflicted, I transgressed; therefore have I kept Thy word.
verses.troparion: "Who giveth me water and springs of tears," cried the Virgin Bride of God, "that I may weep for my sweet Jesus?"

verses.psalm: Good art Thou, O Lord; therefore in Thy goodness teach me Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: "O mountains and valleys, and ye multitudes of mankind, and all creation, weep and lament with me, the Mother of your God."

verses.psalm: The injustice of the proud has been multiplied against me: but I will search out Thy commandments with all my heart.
verses.troparion: "When shall I see Thee, O Savior, eternal light, the joy and gladness of my heart?" cried the Virgin in her bitter grief.

verses.psalm: Their heart has been curdled like milk; but I have meditated on Thy law.
verses.troparion: Thy side was pierced, O Savior, like the rock in the wilderness; but from Thee hath poured forth a stream of living water, for Thou art the Fount of Life.

verses.psalm: It is good for me that Thou hast afflicted me; that I might learn Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: Out of Thy side, as from a single source, there floweth a double stream; and drinking from it we gain immortal life for ourselves.

verses.psalm: The law of Thy mouth is better to me than thousands of gold and silver.
verses.troparion: Of Thine own will, O Word, Thou hast appeared in the tomb as one dead: yet Thou dost live and, as Thou didst foretell, raised up mankind by Thy Resurrection.

verses.psalm: Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit.
verses.troparion: We praise Thee, O Word and God of all, with the Father and Thy most holy Spirit, and with hymns we glorify Thy divine burial.

verses.psalm: Now and ever and unto the ages of ages. Amen.
verses.troparion: We bless thee, O pure Theotokos, and with faith we honor the three-day burial of thy Son and our God.

stasis2._note: Stasis 2 — Tone 5. Psalm 118:73-131 (Yod-Ayin) with troparia. 59 pairs + Glory + Now. Translation: St. Sergius of Radonezh Cathedral.
stasis2.refrain: Right it is to magnify Thee, the Giver of Life, who hath stretched out Thine arms upon the Cross and thereby destroyed the power of the enemy.

verses.psalm: Thy hands have made me, and fashioned me: instruct me, that I may learn Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: Right it is to magnify Thee, the Creator of all, for through Thy Passion we have been granted passionlessness and freedom from corruption.

verses.psalm: They that fear Thee will see me and rejoice: for I have hoped in Thy words.
verses.troparion: The earth trembled with fear, O Savior, and the sun hid itself, beholding Thee O Christ, the never-setting Light, being lowered bodily into the tomb.

verses.psalm: I know, O Lord, that Thy judgments are righteousness, and that Thou in truthfulness hast afflicted me.
verses.troparion: Thou hast slept, O Christ, a life-giving sleep in the tomb, and thereby raised the race of mankind from the slumber of sin.

verses.psalm: Let, I pray Thee, Thy mercy be to comfort me, according to Thy word to Thy servant.
verses.troparion: "Alone among women, only without pain, I gave birth to Thee, my Child", said the pure one. "But now at Thy Passion I suffer unbearable pain."

verses.psalm: Let Thy compassions come to me, that I may live: for Thy law is my meditation.
verses.troparion: Beholding Thee on high O Savior, united inseparably with the Father, yet seeing Thee below laying in the tomb as one dead; the seraphim trembled with fear.

verses.psalm: Let the proud be ashamed; for they transgressed against me unjustly: but I will meditate in Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: The veil of the temple was rent in twain at Thy Crucifixion, O Word, and the celestial lights hid their radiance, when Thou, the Sun, wast hidden beneath the earth.

verses.psalm: Let those that fear Thee, and those that know Thy testimonies, turn to me.
verses.troparion: He Who in the beginning by His will alone put the earth in circular motion, doth now descend breathless beneath the earth. O heaven tremble, at this sight.

verses.psalm: Let mine heart be blameless in Thine ordinances, that I may not be ashamed.
verses.troparion: He who fashioned Adam with His own hand, hath descended beneath the earth, to raise up the fallen race of mankind by His almighty power.

verses.psalm: My soul faints for Thy salvation: I have hoped in Thy words.
verses.troparion: Come, as did the women bearing myrrh, and let us hymn a holy lament to the reposed Christ, that like them we too may hear Him say Rejoice!

verses.psalm: Mine eyes failed in waiting for Thy word, saying, When wilt Thou comfort me?
verses.troparion: Truly art Thou, O Word, the never-failing myrrh: yet the women brought unto Thee, the living God, myrrh, to anoint Thee as one dead.

verses.psalm: For I am become as a bottle in the frost: yet I have not forgotten Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: Thou didst destroy the kingdom of Hades by Thy burial, O Christ: and by Thy death Thou hast slain death, delivering from corruption the race of mankind.

verses.psalm: How many are the days of Thy servant? when wilt Thou execute judgment for me on them that persecute me?
verses.troparion: The source of the river of life, the Wisdom of God, descendeth into the tomb granting life to all those in the depths of Hades.

verses.psalm: Transgressors told me idle tales; but not according to Thy law, O Lord.
verses.troparion: "To renew the broken nature of mortal man, I have been willingly wounded in the flesh by death. O Mother, strike not thy breast in grief."

verses.psalm: All Thy commandments are truth; they persecuted me unjustly; help Thou me.
verses.troparion: O light-bearer of righteousness, Thou hast descended beneath the earth and raised up the dead as if from sleep, dispersing all the darkness of Hades.

verses.psalm: They nearly made an end of me in the earth; but I forsook not Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: The life-giving Seed, twofold in nature, is today sown with tears in the furrows of the earth; but springing forth He shall bring joy to the world.

verses.psalm: Quicken me according to Thy mercy; so shall I keep the testimonies of Thy mouth.
verses.troparion: Adam feared when God walked in Paradise, but now he rejoiceth that God hath descended into Hades. Of old he fell, but now he hath been raised up.

verses.psalm: Thy word, O Lord, abides in heaven for ever.
verses.troparion: Gazing upon Thy body laid in the tomb, O Christ, Thy Mother brought Thee her tears as an offering, and said: "Arise, O Child, as Thou didst foretell."

verses.psalm: Thy truth endures to all generations; Thou hast founded the earth, and it abides.
verses.troparion: The noble Joseph hid Thee reverently in a new tomb, and sang to Thee a fitting hymn, mixing it with his lamentations, O Savior.

verses.psalm: The day continues by Thy arrangement; for all things are Thy servants.
verses.troparion: Seeing Thee, O Word, pierced with nails upon the Cross, Thy Mother was pierced with the nails of bitter grief, and stricken with arrows in the depths of her soul.

verses.psalm: Were it not that Thy law is my meditation, then I should have perished in mine affliction.
verses.troparion: Thy Mother saw Thee, the Sweetness of the world, drink bitter vinegar, whereupon her cheeks became wetted with bitter tears.

verses.psalm: I will never forget Thine ordinances; for with them Thou hast quickened me.
verses.troparion: "I am grievously wounded and my inward being is rent apart, O Word, beholding Thee slain unjustly", said the most pure One weeping.

verses.psalm: I am Thine, save me; for I have sought out Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: "How shall I close Thy sweet eyes and lips, O Word? And how shall I bury Thee as one dead?" cried Joseph trembling.

verses.psalm: Sinners laid wait for me to destroy me; but I understood Thy testimonies.
verses.troparion: Joseph and Nicodemus now sing burial hymns before the reposed Christ; and with them, the seraphim.

verses.psalm: I have seen an end of all perfection; but Thy commandment is very broad.
verses.troparion: The Savior, the Sun of Righteousness, hast set beneath the earth: wherefore the Moon, Thy Mother, hath been engulfed in grief, deprived of beholding Thee any longer.

verses.psalm: How I have loved Thy law, O Lord! it is my meditation all the day.
verses.troparion: Hades trembled, O Savior, upon seeing Thee, the Giver of Life, despoiling him of his wealth and raising up the dead from all the ages.

verses.psalm: Thou hast made me wiser than mine enemies in Thy commandment; for it is mine for ever.
verses.troparion: After night the sun doth brightly shine forth again; and after death, O Word, arise and shine forth in Thy glory, as a bridegroom coming forth from his chamber.

verses.psalm: I have more understanding than all my teachers; for Thy testimonies are my meditation.
verses.troparion: When the earth received Thee in her bosom, O Creator, it shook in fear, O Savior, and quaking, it awoke the dead.

verses.psalm: I understand more than the aged; because I have sought out Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: In a new and wondrous manner Nicodemus and noble Joseph have anointed Thee with spices, crying aloud: "Tremble, O ye earth!"

verses.psalm: I have kept back my feet from every evil way, that I might keep Thy words.
verses.troparion: Thou hast descended, O Creator of light, and with Thee the sun's light hath also set; and creation hath been seized with trembling and proclaiming Thee to be the Fashioner of all.

verses.psalm: I have not declined from Thy judgments; for Thou hast instructed me.
verses.troparion: A stone hewn from rock hath covered the Cornerstone; and a mortal man doth now bury God in a grave as one dead; Tremble, O ye earth.

verses.psalm: How sweet are Thine oracles to my throat! more so than honey to my mouth!
verses.troparion: "Behold the disciple whom Thou hast loved and Thine own Mother, speak to us, O sweetest Child", lamented the pure one weeping.

verses.psalm: I gain understanding by Thy commandments: therefore I have hated every way of unrighteousness.
verses.troparion: Thou art the Giver of Life, O Word, wherefore Thou didst not slay the Jews when stretched out upon the Cross, but rather didst raise their forefathers from the dead.

verses.psalm: Thy law is a lamp to my feet, and a light to my paths.
verses.troparion: At Thy Passion, O Word, there was neither form nor comeliness to be found in Thee: but Thou didst arise in glory, gifting Thy divine effulgence to mankind.

verses.psalm: I have sworn and determined to keep the judgments of Thy righteousness.
verses.troparion: Thou didst descend beneath the earth in the flesh, O never-setting light-bearer, and unable to bear the sight of Thee dead, the sun grew dark at the height of noon-day.

verses.psalm: I have been very greatly afflicted, O Lord: quicken me, according to Thy word.
verses.troparion: The sun and moon darkened themselves, O Savior, like faithful servants clothed in the black robes of mourning.

verses.psalm: Accept, I pray Thee, O Lord, the freewill-offerings of my mouth, and teach me Thy judgments.
verses.troparion: The centurion saw Thee as God, even though Thou didst die, and Joseph cried aloud, "How shall I touch Thee with my hands O my God? I am in fear."

verses.psalm: My soul is continually in Thine hands; and I have not forgotten Thy law.
verses.troparion: Adam slept, but from his side there came forth death; now Thou dost sleep, O Word of God, and from Thy side floweth life into the world.

verses.psalm: Sinners spread a snare for me; but I erred not from Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: Thou didst sleep but a little, and brought the dead to life; Thou didst arise, O Good One, and raise up those who from ages past had fallen asleep.

verses.psalm: I have inherited Thy testimonies for ever; for they are the joy of my heart.
verses.troparion: Taken up from the earth, Thou didst pour forth the wine of salvation, O life-giving Vine, wherefore I glorify Thy Passion and Thy Cross.

verses.psalm: I have inclined my heart to perform Thine ordinances for ever, in return for Thy mercies.
verses.troparion: When the commanders of the noetic hosts on high saw Thee, O Savior, naked, bloodstained and condemned, how could they bear the boldness of Thy crucifiers?

verses.psalm: I have hated transgressors; but I have loved Thy law.
verses.troparion: O ye perverse and crooked Hebrews, ye knew that the temple would be raised again: why then did ye condemn Christ?

verses.psalm: Thou art my helper and my supporter; I have hoped in Thy words.
verses.troparion: In a robe of mockery ye have clothed Him who hath ordered all things, He who hath established the heavens, and wondrously adorned the earth.

verses.psalm: Depart from me, ye evil-doers; for I will search out the commandments of my God.
verses.troparion: Like the pelican Thou wast wounded in Thy side, O Word, restoring Thy dead children to life through the life-giving drops of Thy blood.

verses.psalm: Uphold me according to Thy word, and quicken me; and make me not ashamed of my expectation.
verses.troparion: Of old Joshua made the sun stand still, as he smote the heathen tribes; and Thou didst blot out its light, while casting down the prince of darkness.

verses.psalm: Help me, and I shall be saved; and I will meditate in Thine ordinances continually.
verses.troparion: Without leaving Thy Father's side, O Compassionate One, it was Thy good pleasure, O Christ, to become a man, and descend into Hades.

verses.psalm: Thou hast brought to nought all that depart from Thine ordinances; for their inward thought is unrighteous.
verses.troparion: He who suspended the earth upon the waters is hung upon the Cross. As a breathless corpse He is laid in the earth, and it quaketh in terror, unable to endure His presence.

verses.psalm: I have reckoned all the sinners of the earth as transgressors; therefore have I loved Thy testimonies.
verses.troparion: "Woe is me, my Son!" lamented the Unwedded one saying. "I see now condemned upon the Cross, He whom I had hoped to see enthroned as King."

verses.psalm: Penetrate my flesh with Thy fear; for I am afraid of Thy judgments.
verses.troparion: "Such were the tidings Gabriel brought me when he flew down from heaven: for he said that the Kingdom of my Son Jesus would be eternal."

verses.psalm: I have done judgment and justice; deliver me not up to them that injure me.
verses.troparion: "Alas! the prophecy of Simeon hath been fulfilled: for Thy sword hath pierced my heart, Emmanuel."

verses.psalm: Receive Thy servant for good: let not the proud accuse me falsely.
verses.troparion: Be ashamed, O ye Jews, for the Life-giver hath raised your dead, yet ye slew Him out of envy.

verses.psalm: Mine eyes have failed for Thy salvation, and for the word of Thy righteousness.
verses.troparion: Seeing Thee, O my Christ, the invisible Light, hidden breathless in the tomb, the sun trembled and darkened its light.

verses.psalm: Deal with Thy servant according to Thy mercy, and teach me Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: Thy most immaculate Mother wept bitterly, O Word, when she beheld Thee, the ineffable and beginningless God, lying in the grave.

verses.psalm: I am Thy servant; instruct me, and I shall know Thy testimonies.
verses.troparion: Seeing Thine undefiled death, O Christ, Thy mother cried to Thee in bitter sorrow: "Tarry not, O Life, among the dead."

verses.psalm: It is time for the Lord to work: they have utterly broken Thy law.
verses.troparion: Wicked Hades trembled when it saw Thee, O immortal Sun of glory, and hastily yielded up its prisoners.

verses.psalm: Therefore have I loved Thy commandments more than gold, or the topaz.
verses.troparion: Great and fearful is the sight placed before our eyes: for the Cause of life hath submitted to death, that He may give light and life to all.

verses.psalm: Therefore I directed myself according to all Thy commandments: I have hated every unjust way.
verses.troparion: Thy side hath been pierced, O Master, and Thy hands have been transfixed with nails; healing by Thy wounded side the unbridled lust of our forefathers.

verses.psalm: Thy testimonies are wonderful: therefore my soul has sought them out.
verses.troparion: Of old they wept in every house for Rachel's children; and now the company of disciples with His Mother lament for the Virgin's Son.

verses.psalm: The manifestation of Thy words will enlighten, and instruct the simple.
verses.troparion: With their hands they struck the face of Christ, though it was He who formed man with His hand, and crushed the teeth of the beast.

verses.psalm: I opened my mouth, and drew breath: for I earnestly longed after Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: With our hymns, O Christ, we the faithful now worship Thy Crucifixion and Thy Burial, by which we are set free from death.

verses.psalm: Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit.
verses.troparion: O beginningless God, coeternal Word, and Holy Spirit, strengthen the faith of Orthodox Christians against their enemies, for Thou art exceedingly Good.

verses.psalm: Now and ever and unto the ages of ages. Amen.
verses.troparion: O pure and most immaculate Virgin, who hath given birth to our Life, bring an end to the scandals of the Church, and grant her peace, O good one.

stasis3._note: Stasis 3 — Tone 3. Psalm 118:132-176 (Pe-Tav) with troparia. 45 pairs + Glory + Now. Translation: St. Sergius of Radonezh Cathedral.
stasis3.refrain: Every generation, O my Christ, doth offer praises at Thy burial.

verses.psalm: Look upon me and have mercy upon me, after the manner of them that love Thy name.
verses.troparion: Taking Thee down from the Tree, the Arimathaean lays Thee in a sepulcher.

verses.psalm: Order my steps according to Thy word: and let not any iniquity have dominion over me.
verses.troparion: The Myrrh-bearers drew near, O my Christ, wisely bringing Thee sweet spices.

verses.psalm: Deliver me from the false accusation of men: so will I keep Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: Come ye, and with the whole of creation let us offer a funeral hymn to the Creator.

verses.psalm: Cause Thy face to shine upon Thy servant: and teach me Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: With the Myrrhbearers let us all consciously anoint the Living One as one dead.

verses.psalm: Mine eyes have been bathed in streams of water, because I kept not Thy law.
verses.troparion: O thrice-blessed Joseph, bury the body of Christ, the Life-Giver.

verses.psalm: Righteous art Thou, O Lord, and upright are Thy judgments.
verses.troparion: Those whom He fed with manna have lifted up their heel against their Benefactor.

verses.psalm: Thou hast commanded righteousness and perfect truth, as Thy testimonies.
verses.troparion: Those whom He fed with manna, offer to the Savior vinegar and gall.

verses.psalm: Thy zeal has quite wasted me: because mine enemies have forgotten Thy words.
verses.troparion: O the madness of those who killed the prophets and slew Christ!

verses.psalm: Thy word has been very fully tried; and Thy servant loves it.
verses.troparion: Like a mindless servant, the disciple hath betrayed the abyss of wisdom.

verses.psalm: I am young and despised: yet I have not forgotten Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: Judas the traitor hath sold his Redeemer and made himself a captive.

verses.psalm: Thy righteousness is an everlasting righteousness, and Thy law is truth.
verses.troparion: According to Solomon, the mouth of the transgressing Hebrews is likened to a deep pit.

verses.psalm: Afflictions and distresses found me: but Thy commandments were my meditation.
verses.troparion: In the crooked paths of the transgressing Hebrews there are thorns and snares.

verses.psalm: Thy testimonies are an everlasting righteousness: instruct me, and I shall live.
verses.troparion: Joseph with Nicodemus bury the Creator with honors befitting the reposed.

verses.psalm: I cried with my whole heart; hear me, O Lord: I will search out Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: O Life-giver and Savior, to Thy power be glory, for Thou hast destroyed Hades!

verses.psalm: I cried to Thee; save me, and I will keep Thy testimonies.
verses.troparion: Upon seeing Thee lying dead, O Word, the most pure One wept with a mother's grief.

verses.psalm: I arose before the dawn, and cried: I hoped in Thy words.
verses.troparion: "O my sweet springtime, my sweetest Child, where hath all Thy comeliness gone?"

verses.psalm: Mine eyes prevented the dawn, that I might meditate on Thine oracles.
verses.troparion: Thine all-pure Mother lamented deeply upon seeing Thou, O Word, as one dead.

verses.psalm: Hear my voice, O Lord, according to Thy mercy; quicken me according to Thy judgment.
verses.troparion: The women came with myrrh to anoint Christ, the Divine Myrrh.

verses.psalm: They have drawn nigh who persecuted me unlawfully; and they are far removed from Thy law.
verses.troparion: By dying, O my God, Thou didst put death to death by Thy divine power.

verses.psalm: Thou art near, O Lord; and all Thy ways are truth.
verses.troparion: The deceiver is deceived, and those whom he misled are set free by Thy Wisdom, O my God.

verses.psalm: I have known of old concerning Thy testimonies, that Thou hast founded them for ever.
verses.troparion: The traitor was cast down to the deepest depths of Hades, and into the pit of corruption.

verses.psalm: Look upon mine affliction, and rescue me; for I have not forgotten Thy law.
verses.troparion: Thorns and snares are the path of the thrice-wretched foolish Judas.

verses.psalm: Plead my cause, and ransom me: quicken me because of Thy word.
verses.troparion: All who crucified Thee shall be destroyed together, O Word, Thou Son of God and King of all.

verses.psalm: Salvation is far from sinners: for they have not searched out Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: In the pit of corruption shall all the men of blood be destroyed together.

verses.psalm: Thy mercies, O Lord, are many: quicken me according to Thy judgment.
verses.troparion: O Son of God and King of all, my God and my Creator, how hast Thou accepted suffering?

verses.psalm: Many are they that persecute me and oppress me: but I have not declined from Thy testimonies.
verses.troparion: As the mother of a foal, she gazed on Thee hanging upon the Tree.

verses.psalm: I beheld men acting foolishly, and I pined away; for they kept not Thine oracles.
verses.troparion: Joseph with Nicodemus bury a body that is the Source of life.

verses.psalm: Behold, I have loved Thy commandments, O Lord: quicken me in Thy mercy.
verses.troparion: Pierced to the heart, the Virgin shed warm tears and bewailed her child.

verses.psalm: The beginning of Thy words is truth; and all the judgments of Thy righteousness endure for ever.
verses.troparion: "O Light of mine eyes, my sweetest Child, how art Thou hidden now in the sepulchre?"

verses.psalm: Princes persecuted me without a cause, but my heart feared because of Thy words.
verses.troparion: "Weep not, O Mother, for I suffer this to free Adam and Eve."

verses.psalm: I will exult because of Thine oracles, as one that finds much spoil.
verses.troparion: "O my Son, I glorify Thine extreme compassion, for which cause Thou hast suffered."

verses.psalm: I hate and abhor unrighteousness; but I love Thy law.
verses.troparion: Thou hast drunk vinegar and gall, O Compassionate One, to redeem us from the forbidden fruit of old.

verses.psalm: Seven times in a day have I praised Thee because of the judgments of Thy righteousness.
verses.troparion: Thou art nailed upon the Cross, who of old didst shelter Thy people with a pillar of cloud.

verses.psalm: Great peace have they that love Thy law: and there is no stumbling-block to them.
verses.troparion: The Myrrh-bearers came to Thy tomb O Savior, bringing Thee Myrrh.

verses.psalm: I waited for Thy salvation, O Lord, and have loved Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: Rise up, O Compassionate One, and raise us from the depths of Hades.

verses.psalm: My soul has kept Thy testimonies, and loved them exceedingly.
verses.troparion: "Arise, O Giver of Life", the Mother who bore Thee said with flowing tears.

verses.psalm: I have kept Thy commandments and Thy testimonies; for all my ways are before Thee, O Lord.
verses.troparion: Make haste to arise, O Word, and take away the sorrow of the pure one who bore Thee.

verses.psalm: Let my supplication come near before Thee, O Lord; instruct me according to Thine oracle.
verses.troparion: All the powers of heaven have been filled with fear and awe upon seeing Thee dead.

verses.psalm: Let my petition come in before Thee, O Lord; deliver me according to Thine oracle.
verses.troparion: With love and fear we honor Thy Passion: grant us the remission of our sins.

verses.psalm: Let my lips utter a hymn, when Thou shalt have taught me Thine ordinances.
verses.troparion: Strange and dreadful is the vision! O Word of God, how art Thou now hidden by the earth?

verses.psalm: Let my tongue utter Thine oracles; for all Thy commandments are righteous.
verses.troparion: Joseph once fled with Thee, O Savior, and now another Joseph doth bury Thee.

verses.psalm: Let Thine hand be prompt to save me; for I have chosen Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: Thy most-holy Mother weepeth for Thee lamenting, O my Savior, as One dead.

verses.psalm: I have longed after Thy salvation, O Lord; and Thy law is my meditation.
verses.troparion: The noetical powers tremble at Thy strange and fearful burial, O Fashioner of all that is.

verses.psalm: My soul shall live, and shall praise Thee; and Thy judgments shall help me.
verses.troparion: Early in the morning the myrrh-bearers came to Thee and poured myrrh upon Thy tomb.

verses.psalm: I have gone astray like a lost sheep; seek Thy servant; for I have not forgotten Thy commandments.
verses.troparion: By Thy Resurrection grant peace to the Church and salvation to Thy people.

verses.psalm: Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit.
verses.troparion: O my God in Trinity, the Father, the Son and the Spirit, have mercy upon the world.

verses.psalm: Now and ever and unto the ages of ages. Amen.
verses.troparion: Grant unto us thy servants O Virgin, that we be deemed worthy to behold the Resurrection of thy Son.

smallLitanies._note: Small litanies (ektenias) after each stasis. Each has a different priestly exclamation.

petitions.opening: Again and again, in peace, let us pray to the Lord.
petitions.helpUs: Help us, save us, have mercy on us, and keep us, O God, by Thy grace.
petitions.commemoration: Calling to remembrance our most holy, most pure, most blessed, glorious Lady Theotokos and Ever-Virgin Mary with all the saints, let us commit ourselves and one another and all our life unto Christ our God.

afterStasis1.exclamation: For blessed is Thy Name and glorified is Thy Kingdom; of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit, now and ever, and unto the ages of ages.

afterStasis2.exclamation: For holy art Thou, our God, who restest upon the glorious throne of the cherubim, and unto Thee do we send up glory, together with Thine unoriginate Father and Thy most holy, good and life-creating Spirit, now and ever, and unto the ages of ages.

afterStasis3._note: Some traditions omit the small litany after Stasis 3 and proceed directly to the Evlogetaria. OCA practice typically includes it.
afterStasis3.exclamation: For Thou art the King of peace, O Christ our God, and unto Thee we send up glory, together with Thine unoriginate Father, and Thy most holy, good and life-creating Spirit, now and ever, and unto the ages of ages.

evlogetaria._note: Evlogetaria of the Dead, adapted for Holy Saturday. Tone 5. Sung after the three stases.
evlogetaria.refrain: Blessed art Thou, O Lord; teach me Thy statutes.

troparia.verse: Blessed art Thou, O Lord; teach me Thy statutes.
troparia.text:
The assembly of angels were amazed,
beholding Thee numbered among the dead;
yet, O Savior, destroying the stronghold of death,
and with Thyself raising up Adam,
and freeing all from Hades.

troparia.verse: Blessed art Thou, O Lord; teach me Thy statutes.
troparia.text:
"Why mingle ye myrrh with tears of pity, O ye women disciples?"
Thus did the radiant angel address the myrrh-bearing women within the tomb;
"behold the tomb and understand,
for the Savior hath arisen from the tomb."

troparia.verse: Blessed art Thou, O Lord; teach me Thy statutes.
troparia.text:
Very early the myrrh-bearing women hastened unto Thy tomb, lamenting,
but the angel stood before them and said:
"the time for lamentation is passed, weep not,
but tell of the Resurrection to the apostles."

troparia.verse: Blessed art Thou, O Lord; teach me Thy statutes.
troparia.text:
The myrrh-bearing women, with myrrh came to Thy tomb, O Savior, bewailing,
but the angel addressed them, saying:
"Why number ye the living among the dead,
for as God He is risen from the tomb."

troparia.verse: Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit.
troparia.text:
Let us worship the Father, and His Son, and the Holy Spirit,
the Holy Trinity, one in essence,
crying with the Seraphim: Holy, Holy, Holy art Thou, O Lord.

troparia.verse: Now and ever and unto the ages of ages. Amen.
troparia.text:
In bringing forth the Giver of life, thou hast delivered Adam from sin, O Virgin,
and hast brought joy to Eve instead of sorrow;
and those fallen from life have thereunto been restored,
by Him Who of thee was incarnate, God and man.

evlogetaria.alleluia: Alleluia, alleluia, alleluia. Glory to Thee, O God. (×3)

canon._note: The Great Canon of Holy Saturday. Abbreviated — full odes would be very lengthy.
canon.rubric: The Canon of Holy Saturday is chanted here. The first, third, fourth, and ODES of this canon are the work of Mark the Monk, Bishop of Hydrous, and the last four ODES are by Kosmas of the Holy City. The Irmoi are the work of the nun Kassiani.

kontakion.text:
He Who closed the abyss is beheld as dead,
and as a corpse the Immortal One hath been wrapped in linen with sweet spices and laid in a tomb.
The women come to anoint Him with myrrh,
weeping bitterly and crying:
"This is the most blessed Sabbath on which Christ sleepeth,
but on the third day He shall rise again."

ikos.text:
He Who sustaineth all things was lifted up upon the Cross,
and all creation wept, seeing Him hanging naked on the Tree.
The sun hid its rays, and the stars cast aside their light;
the earth shook in great fear, and the sea fled,
the rocks were rent asunder, and the graves of many were opened,
and the bodies of the saints arose.
Hades groaned below, and the Jews conspired to spread slander against Christ's Resurrection.
But the women cried aloud:
"This is the most blessed Sabbath on which Christ sleepeth,
but on the third day He shall rise again."

exaposteilarion.label: Exaposteilarion
exaposteilarion.text: Holy is the Lord our God!
exaposteilarion._note: Sung three times, with the verse 'For He is holy' and 'Exalt the Lord our God.'

lauds._note: Lauds stichera. Tone 2. Sung with 'Let every breath praise the Lord.'

hymns.verse: Let every breath praise the Lord. Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise Him in the highest!
hymns.text:
Every creature was altered by Thy Passion, O Lord;
all things suffered with Thee, knowing that Thou holdest all things together.

hymns.verse: Praise Him for His mighty acts; praise Him according to the multitude of His greatness!
hymns.text:
The Giver of the Law lies dead; a lawless people has condemned the Lawgiver.
O new wonders! The Judge is judged, yet He wills to suffer and to die for the salvation of the world.

hymns.verse: Praise Him with the sound of the trumpet; praise Him with the psaltery and harp!
hymns.text:
O Lord, when the myrrh-bearing women stood at Thy tomb,
and the soldiers guarded Thy most pure Body,
Thou didst plunder hell and condemn the demons,
rising as Victor on the third day.

hymns.verse: Praise Him with timbrel and dance; praise Him with strings and instruments!
hymns.text:
O Lord, the hell that had swallowed the Cornerstone
has released its dead;
and the tomb has given up for judgment those whom it held of old.
The Giver of Life has shut up death, and the Lord has poured His light on all.

glory.text:
Today a tomb holds Him Who holds all creation in the hollow of His hand.
A stone covers Him Who covers the heavens with virtue.
Life sleeps, and hell trembles, and Adam is set free from his bonds.
Glory to Thy dispensation, whereby, having accomplished all things,
Thou hast granted us an eternal Sabbath,
Thy most holy Resurrection from the dead!

now.text:
Most blessed art thou, O Virgin Theotokos,
for through Him Who was incarnate of thee,
Hades is led captive,
Adam recalled, the curse destroyed,
Eve freed, death slain,
and we are given life.
Wherefore, we cry out in praise:
Blessed art Thou, O Christ our God, Who art thus well pleased! Glory to Thee!

greatDoxology._note: The Great Doxology is SUNG (not read) at the Lamentations service.
greatDoxology.text:
Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will among men.

We praise Thee, we bless Thee, we worship Thee, we glorify Thee, we give thanks to Thee for Thy great glory.

O Lord God, heavenly King, God the Father Almighty; O Lord, the only-begotten Son, Jesus Christ; and O Holy Spirit.

O Lord God, Lamb of God, Son of the Father, that takest away the sin of the world, have mercy on us; Thou that takest away the sins of the world, receive our prayer; Thou that sittest at the right hand of the Father, have mercy on us.

For Thou only art holy, Thou only art the Lord, Jesus Christ, to the glory of God the Father. Amen.

Every day will I bless Thee, and I will praise Thy Name forever and ever.

Vouchsafe, O Lord, to keep us this day without sin.

Blessed art Thou, O Lord, the God of our fathers, and praised and glorified is Thy Name forever. Amen.

Let Thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, as we have set our hope on Thee.

Blessed art Thou, O Lord; teach me Thy statutes.
Blessed art Thou, O Master; make me to understand Thy statutes.
Blessed art Thou, O Holy One; enlighten me with Thy statutes.

Thy mercy, O Lord, endureth forever. O despise not the works of Thy hands.

To Thee belongeth worship, to Thee belongeth praise, to Thee belongeth glory: to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, now and ever and unto ages of ages. Amen.

trisagion._note: Sung during the procession with the Epitaphios, after the Great Doxology.
trisagion.text:
Holy God, Holy Mighty, Holy Immortal, have mercy on us. (×3)

Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, now and ever and unto ages of ages. Amen.

Holy Immortal, have mercy on us.

Holy God, Holy Mighty, Holy Immortal, have mercy on us.

procession._note: The Epitaphios procession takes place after the Great Doxology. The Trisagion is sung during the procession.
procession.rubric: The Great Doxology is sung. At its conclusion the priest takes up the Epitaphios (burial shroud of Christ) and carries it in procession around the church, while the Trisagion is chanted.

prophecy._note: After the procession, the prophecy from Ezekiel is read.
prophecy.label: Prophecy of Ezekiel
prophecy.text: The hand of the Lord was upon me, and carried me out in the Spirit of the Lord, and set me down in the midst of the valley which was full of bones, and caused me to pass by them round about: and, behold, there were very many in the open valley; and, lo, they were very dry. And He said unto me: Son of man, can these bones live? And I answered: O Lord God, Thou knowest. Again He said unto me: Prophesy upon these bones, and say unto them: O ye dry bones, hear the word of the Lord. Thus saith the Lord God unto these bones: Behold, I will cause breath to enter into you, and ye shall live: and I will lay sinews upon you, and will bring up flesh upon you, and cover you with skin, and put breath in you, and ye shall live; and ye shall know that I am the Lord. So I prophesied as I was commanded: and as I prophesied, there was a noise, and behold a shaking, and the bones came together, bone to his bone. And when I beheld, lo, the sinews and the flesh came up upon them, and the skin covered them above: but there was no breath in them. Then said He unto me: Prophesy unto the wind, prophesy, son of man, and say to the wind: Thus saith the Lord God: Come from the four winds, O breath, and breathe upon these slain, that they may live. So I prophesied as He commanded me, and the breath came into them, and they lived, and stood up upon their feet, an exceeding great army. Then He said unto me: Son of man, these bones are the whole house of Israel: behold, they say: Our bones are dried, and our hope is lost; we are cut off for our parts. Therefore prophesy and say unto them: Thus saith the Lord God: Behold, O My people, I will open your graves, and cause you to come up out of your graves, and bring you into the land of Israel. And ye shall know that I am the Lord, when I have opened your graves, O My people, and brought you up out of your graves, and shall put My Spirit in you, and ye shall live, and I shall place you in your own land: then shall ye know that I the Lord have spoken it, and performed it, saith the Lord.

prokeimenon.refrain: Arise, O Lord, help us, and redeem us for Thy mercy's sake.
prokeimenon.verse: O God, we have heard with our ears, and our fathers have told us.

epistle.text:
Brethren: Know ye not that a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump? Purge out therefore the old leaven, that ye may be a new lump, as ye are unleavened. For even Christ our Passover is sacrificed for us. Therefore let us keep the feast, not with old leaven, neither with the leaven of malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.

Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree: that the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Jesus Christ; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.

verses: Arise, O Lord my God; let Thy hand be lifted high; forget not Thy poor forever.
I will give thanks unto Thee, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will tell of all Thy wondrous works.

gospel.label: The Sealing of the Tomb
gospel.text: Now the next day, that followed the day of the preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees came together unto Pilate, saying: Sir, we remember that that deceiver said, while He was yet alive: After three days I will rise again. Command therefore that the sepulchre be made sure until the third day, lest His disciples come by night, and steal Him away, and say unto the people: He is risen from the dead; so the last error shall be worse than the first. Pilate said unto them: Ye have a watch; go your way, make it as sure as ye can. So they went, and made the sepulchre sure, sealing the stone, and setting a watch.

dismissal.text: May He Who for our salvation endured the spitting, and the scourging, and the buffeting, and the Cross, and death, and was laid in the tomb, Christ our true God, through the prayers of His all-pure Mother, and of all the saints, have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loveth mankind.
